{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sanmyōji Kofun | native_name =三明寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sanmyoji Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sanmyōji Kofun entry | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sanmyōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the burial mounds in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genmon-2.jpg|Entry File:Sanmyoji Kofun, sokuheki-2.jpg|Burial chamber construction File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|'Monolithic rear wall and remnants of sarcophagus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sanmyōji Kofun | native_name =三明寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sanmyoji Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sanmyōji Kofun entry | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sanmyōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the burial mounds in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sanmyōji Kofun | native_name =三明寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sanmyoji Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sanmyōji Kofun entry | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sanmyōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the burial mounds in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genmon-2.jpg|Entry File:Sanmyoji Kofun, sokuheki-2.jpg|Burial chamber construction File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|'Monolithic rear wall and remnants of sarcophagus </gallery>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the burial mounds in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sanmyōji Kofun | native_name =三明寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sanmyoji Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sanmyōji Kofun entry | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sanmyōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the burial mounds in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sanmyōji Kofun | native_name =三明寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sanmyoji Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sanmyōji Kofun entry | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sanmyōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the burial mounds in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genmon-2.jpg|Entry File:Sanmyoji Kofun, sokuheki-2.jpg|Burial chamber construction File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|'Monolithic rear wall and remnants of sarcophagus </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sanmyōji Kofun | native_name =三明寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sanmyoji Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sanmyōji Kofun entry | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sanmyōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the burial mounds in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sanmyōji Kofun | native_name =三明寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sanmyoji Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sanmyōji Kofun entry | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sanmyōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the burial mounds in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sanmyōji Kofun | native_name =三明寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sanmyoji Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sanmyōji Kofun entry | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sanmyōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the burial mounds in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the burial mounds in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sanmyōji Kofun | native_name =三明寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sanmyoji Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sanmyōji Kofun entry | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sanmyōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the burial mounds in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sanmyōji Kofun | native_name =三明寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sanmyoji Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sanmyōji Kofun entry | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sanmyōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the burial mounds in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genmon-2.jpg|Entry File:Sanmyoji Kofun, sokuheki-2.jpg|Burial chamber construction File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|'Monolithic rear wall and remnants of sarcophagus </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sanmyōji Kofun | native_name =三明寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sanmyoji Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sanmyōji Kofun entry | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sanmyōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the burial mounds in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genmon-2.jpg|Entry File:Sanmyoji Kofun, sokuheki-2.jpg|Burial chamber construction File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|'Monolithic rear wall and remnants of sarcophagus </gallery>

It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the burial mounds in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the burial mounds in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the Kofuns in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genmon-2.jpg|Entry File:Sanmyoji Kofun, sokuheki-2.jpg|Burial chamber construction File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|'Monolithic rear wall and remnants of sarcophagus </gallery>

The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the Kofuns in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genmon-2.jpg|Entry File:Sanmyoji Kofun, sokuheki-2.jpg|Burial chamber construction File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|'Monolithic rear wall and remnants of sarcophagus </gallery>

It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Sanmyōji Kofun | native_name =三明寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sanmyoji Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sanmyōji Kofun entry | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sanmyōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the Kofuns in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Sanmyōji Kofun | native_name =三明寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sanmyoji Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sanmyōji Kofun entry | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sanmyōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the Kofuns in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genmon-2.jpg|Entry File:Sanmyoji Kofun, sokuheki-2.jpg|Burial chamber construction File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|'Monolithic rear wall and remnants of sarcophagus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Sanmyoji Kofun}} *[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/9FC90037D0BDF0634925796F0007FCE6?OpenDocument Cultural properties of Tottori] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/5/#00 Kurayoshi City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Sanmyōji Kofun | native_name =三明寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sanmyoji Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sanmyōji Kofun entry | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sanmyōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the Kofuns in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genmon-2.jpg|Entry File:Sanmyoji Kofun, sokuheki-2.jpg|Burial chamber construction File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|'Monolithic rear wall and remnants of sarcophagus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Sanmyōji Kofun | native_name =三明寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sanmyoji Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sanmyōji Kofun entry | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sanmyōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th to early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the Kofuns in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genmon-2.jpg|Entry File:Sanmyoji Kofun, sokuheki-2.jpg|Burial chamber construction File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|'Monolithic rear wall and remnants of sarcophagus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Sanmyōji Kofun'''|三明寺古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iwaojō neighborhood of the city of [[Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1931. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/161958|title= 三明寺古墳|trans-title= Sanmyōji Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> One of the Kofuns in the Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Sanmyōji Kofun is located on the south side of the Mukaiyama Hills that spreads out to the north of central Kurayoshi. It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. It has a diameter of 18 meters and a height of six meters. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is believed to be an {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mound, although it has been scraped away and deformed and may possibly have originally been a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style square mound, or even polygonal-shaped. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 8.3 meters in length, 3.7 meters in depth, and 3.1 meters in height with a huge monolith forming the rear wall. In addition, there is an enclosure made of stone slabs adjacent to the back wall of the burial chamber. This antechamber construction is more commonly found in the [[Kyushu]] region of Japan. At the time of construction, it is presumed that there was a cover stone on top of the stone enclosure, and that the floor was covered with small pebbles. The burial chamber is known to have been open since ancient times, so no [[grave goods]] have been recovered. Based on the structure of the stone chamber, the tumulus is believed to have been built around the end of the 6th century or the beginning of the 7th century, or at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located a ten-minute car from [[Kurayoshi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genmon-2.jpg|Entry File:Sanmyoji Kofun, sokuheki-2.jpg|Burial chamber construction File:Sanmyoji Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|'Monolithic rear wall and remnants of sarcophagus </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]
